name: supervision
tagline: The glue code of computer vision, written once, properly
description: Roboflow's model-agnostic utility library: reusable building blocks for detection, tracking, annotation, dataset conversion, and video analytics. Connects to models from Ultralytics, Transformers, and MMDetection, and provides annotators, multi-object tracking, zone counting, and speed estimation.
why: The toolkit that sits on top of any detector so teams stop rewriting drawing, tracking, and dataset plumbing. MIT-licensed — a commercially clean layer over an ecosystem where the most famous models are AGPL.

setup.requirements: Python 3.10+, pip install supervision, plus any detection model

license.notes: The library is MIT; it merely connects to external models, some of which (Ultralytics YOLO) are AGPL — your model choice sets your obligations.

limitations: Not a model itself — throughput and accuracy come from whatever you plug into it.
take: Every field has one library everyone quietly depends on. In vision analytics, you're looking at it.
